name: Las Arrodeas - El Pinatar Trail
name_original: Las Arrodeas - El Pinatar – circular desde Olula del Río (PR-A 328)
description: Hike the moderate 8.6-mile Las Arrodeas - El Pinatar Trail, featuring a historic path, pine forests, and panoramic views from Mirador del Pi
region_intro: Hiking around Olula Del Río offers diverse landscapes within the Almanzora Valley in Almería, Spain. The region is characterized by geological formations, including narrow gorges like Garganta del Maimón, and prominent vertical mountains such as Piedra Ver. Hikers can explore a network of trails across hills like Fraile and Hondo, or follow the accessible Almanzora Greenway, which traces a former railway line. Best hiking trails in Olula Del Río The most popular hiking route is Las Arrodeas - El Pinatar – circular from Olula del Río (PR-A 328), a 8.6 miles (13.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 13 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain and views of the surrounding countryside. Another top favourite among local hikers is World's largest mortar – Fuente de las Focas of Macael loop from Macael, an easy 3.3 miles (5.2 km) path. This route features gentle gradients and passes by a notable local landmark. Local hikers also love the Cantoria Tunnel – Cantoria Tunnel loop from Cantoria, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through varied landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 23 minutes. Hiking around Olula Del Río is defined by narrow gorges, vertical mountains, and greenways, with routes offering access to the Almanzora River and surrounding hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to different ability levels. The routes in Olula Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 70 hikers have used komoot to explore Olula Del Río's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Olula Del Río?
A: There are 19 hiking trails around Olula Del Río, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You'll find 4 easy routes, 14 moderate routes, and 1 difficult route.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Olula Del Río?
A: The terrain around Olula Del Río is diverse, featuring geological formations like narrow gorges, prominent vertical mountains, and accessible greenways. You can expect varied landscapes, from the sculpted walls of &lt;strong&gt;Garganta del Maimón&lt;/strong&gt; to the paths around hills like Fraile and Hondo, and the flat, former railway line of the &lt;strong&gt;Almanzora Greenway&lt;/strong&gt;.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Olula Del Río?
A: Yes, there are several family-friendly options. The region offers 4 easy routes, which are generally suitable for families. A good example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14600272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;World's largest mortar – Fuente de las Focas of Macael loop from Macael&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.3-mile (5.2 km) path with gentle gradients.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Olula Del Río?
A: Many trails in the Olula Del Río area are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to check specific route details for any restrictions. The &lt;strong&gt;Almanzora Greenway&lt;/strong&gt; is generally accessible and a good option for walks with dogs, offering a flat and easy path.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for on the trails?
A: Olula Del Río is rich in natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the dramatic geological formations of &lt;strong&gt;Garganta del Maimón&lt;/strong&gt;, the emblematic vertical mountain &lt;strong&gt;Piedra Ver de Olula&lt;/strong&gt;, or the scenic landscapes around the Hills of Fraile and Hondo. Additionally, you might encounter historical structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5037673" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantoria Tunnel&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5037637" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aqueduct near Purchena&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as circular loops. A popular moderate circular route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e984324837/las-arrodeas-el-pinatar-circular-from-olula-del-rio-pr-a-328" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Arrodeas - El Pinatar – circular from Olula del Río (PR-A 328)&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 8.6 miles (13.8 km) and offers varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Olula Del Río?
A: Olula Del Río, located in Almería, Spain, generally offers pleasant hiking conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn are ideal, with milder temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended, while winters are typically mild, making it suitable for hiking as well.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads?
A: Parking availability varies by trailhead. For routes starting directly from towns like Olula del Río or Macael, you can often find street parking or designated parking areas. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check local information or maps for parking facilities.

Q: Are there any cafes or places to eat near the hiking trails?
A: Olula Del Río and nearby villages like Macael and Cantoria offer various amenities, including cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel before or after your hike. Many routes start or pass through these settlements, making it convenient to find refreshments.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Olula Del Río?
A: The hiking routes in Olula Del Río are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the unique geological formations like gorges and vertical mountains, and the variety of trails catering to different abilities.

Q: Are there any permits required for hiking in the Olula Del Río area?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for hiking on the established trails around Olula Del Río. Most routes are publicly accessible. However, always respect private property and any local signage you may encounter.

Q: Can I reach the trailheads using public transport?
A: Public transport options to specific trailheads might be limited. While Olula Del Río and surrounding towns have some bus services, direct access to more remote trail starts may require a car. The &lt;strong&gt;Almanzora Greenway&lt;/strong&gt;, being a former railway line, might have sections more accessible from towns with public transport connections.
tips:
The Senda de las Arrodeas is a circular route of approximately 13.7 kilometers that connects Las Arrodeas with El Pinatar in Olula del Río, Almería. Along this route, hikers can enjoy beautiful natural landscapes and panoramic views.
I hope you enjoy exploring the Senda de las Arrodeas! 🌿🏞️

Hermitage built at the foot of Cerro Almirez in the Sierra de las Estancias. The decision that the hermitage is located in that area is because it is located on the industrial estate. When the work was projected, this area had a lot of production and abundant workers, and that was the reason why it was done in that place, so that the Christ would offer them his protection. The first stone of the hermitage was placed by the residents who at that time were the oldest in the town, José Mena and Carmen González, who were then 103 years old.

In the square of the hermitage is the image of the Christ of the Good Death, a sculpture carved in Macael White Marble in one piece and of singular beauty.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Las Arrodeas - El Pinatar Trail?
faqs.answer: This circular hike is approximately 13.8 kilometers long. Most hikers complete it in about 4 hours and 12 minutes, depending on their pace and how long they stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers varied terrain. The initial kilometers are considered somewhat challenging due to continuous ascent and zig-zag sections on the old Senda de las Arrodeas. After this, it transitions to a more pleasant forest track, with some asphalt sections appearing towards the end of the route.

faqs.question: Where can I park and access the trailhead for this route?
faqs.answer: The Las Arrodeas - El Pinatar Trail is a circular route starting and ending in Olula del Río. While specific parking details aren't provided, you would typically find parking within the town to begin your hike.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll traverse the historic Senda de las Arrodeas, an ancient local path. The route also passes by the Hermitage of Christ of the Good Death. A major highlight is the Mirador del Pinatar (also known as Mirador del Tesoro) at the highest point, offering panoramic views.

faqs.question: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots on the trail?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! The Mirador del Pinatar, located at approximately 1,071 meters, provides impressive panoramic views of the Almanzora Valley. On clear days, you might even spot the coastlines of Garrucha and Vera in the distance. There are also picnic tables at this high point to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: Is the Las Arrodeas - El Pinatar Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific restrictions are not mentioned, many trails in this region are dog-friendly. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near agricultural land, and to always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: There is no indication that permits or entrance fees are required to hike the Las Arrodeas - El Pinatar Trail (PR-A 328). It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the varied terrain and potential for heat in Almería, spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike this trail. The weather is milder, making the ascents more comfortable and the views more enjoyable. Summers can be very hot, and winters might bring colder temperatures at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Las Arrodeas - El Pinatar Trail (PR-A 328) covers 100% of its own route. It also shares about 15% of its path with the Cuesta de las Arrodeas trail.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: The trail is officially graded as 'moderate'. While it is a circular route, the initial kilometers involve a continuous and somewhat hard ascent with zig-zag sections. Beginners with a reasonable level of fitness should be able to manage it, but those new to hiking might find the start challenging.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like picnic areas along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are recreational amenities available. You'll find picnic tables at the highest point of the trail, near the Mirador del Pinatar, perfect for a break. There's also a dedicated picnic area known as the Merendero del Pinatar.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of Christ of the Good Death
